basics.name: Sergio Charles
basics.label: Mathematician
basics.summary: A student of mathematics and statistics, interested in creating models of the real-world.

work.name: QuantRes Asset Management
work.summary: Trading in the global futures, equities and foreign exchange markets.

work.name: SS&C Alps Funds
work.summary:
• Research and implement statistical arbitrage strategies using mean-version and factor models for trading on the portfolio optimization team. • Construct macroeconomic models for each market sector, using growth, inflation, commodities, and
      interest rate indicators to analyze deviations between the best fit model and price action of market segments, in order to seek arbitrage opportunities for trading.

work.name: IRIS Lab: Intelligence through Robotic Interaction at Scale
work.summary:
Conducted research on model-based Autonomous Reinforcement Learning (ARL) algorithms using
      aligned latent models to train agents in the reset-free, non-episodic setting, under Prof. Chelsea Finn in
      the Stanford Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (SAIL).

work.name: Nvidia
work.summary:
• Conducted research on Neural Machine Translation distillation, including Hinton, DistilBERT, and
      sequence-level interpolation knowledge distillation for Transformer NMT model architectures as part of
      the Conversational AI team.
      • Developed a semi-supervised, hybrid distillation combining word-level and sequence-level interpolation
      distillation for NMT, and implemented NMT distillation as a part of the open-sourced
      DistillationModelPT framework in NeMo.

work.name: Stanford Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (SAIL)
work.summary:
Completed AI for Climate Change Bootcamp advised by Prof. Andrew Ng. Developed weakly
      supervised global well-pad detection models with Detectron2, leveraging self-training and distillation
      with a noisy student approach [Xie et al.,
        2020
      ] for remote sensing in geologic structural basins.

awards.title: Prize for CS224N Best Custom Project Report
awards.summary: Awarded prize for best project in Stanford's graduate Natural Language Processing course, CS224N Winter 2021.

awards.title: CURIS Research Fellowship
awards.summary: Awarded department fellowship for Computer Science Research.
